features.summary: Nike reported flat Q4 revenue with strong performance growth offset by lifestyle declines, particularly impacting digital channels. Full-year revenue grew 1% currency-neutral with 15% EPS growth. The company is executing a multi-year comeback strategy focused on sport, innovation acceleration, and marketplace expansion. Management is proactively managing classic franchise supply to create capacity for new product launches, which will pressure near-term results but position for long-term growth.
features.management_outlook: Nike expects fiscal 2025 revenue to decline mid-single digits with first half down high-single digits, driven by aggressive classic franchise management and digital headwinds. The company anticipates meaningful sequential improvement in the second half as new product innovations scale. Management plans to double new product business contribution from start of fiscal 2024 to end of fiscal 2025, with significant innovation pipeline including Pegasus Premium, Vomero 18, and expanded Dynamic Air platform launching in spring 2025.
features.risks: Key risks include continued lifestyle franchise declines, particularly on Nike Digital which fell 10% in Q4, and macro uncertainty in Greater China with double-digit brick-and-mortar traffic declines. Foreign exchange headwinds worsened, creating additional one-point revenue impact. The company faces execution risk in scaling new innovations quickly enough to offset planned reductions in classic franchises, with channel mix shifts creating margin pressure in the transition period.
features.key_drivers: Performance business drove double-digit growth across all segments, with basketball, fitness, and running showing strong momentum. New innovations like Air Max DN became top-10 lifestyle franchise and Pegasus 41 launched with strong sell-through. The company is investing nearly $1 billion in consumer-facing activities in fiscal 2025, including enhanced ground game, sports marketing, and brand campaigns around Paris Olympics. Wholesale order books show double-digit growth in key categories like North American running specialty.
